'''Brad Dwyer''' (born 28 April 1993) is an English [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|hk}} for [[Salford RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship]].

He previously played for [[Leeds Rhinos]] and [[Hull FC]] in the [[Super League]], playing on loan from [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at the [[Swinton Lions]] and the [[London Broncos]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], and the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the Super League. He has also spent time on loan from [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] at [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

==Background==
Dwyer was born in [[Wigan]], [[Greater Manchester, England]]. His cousin [[Taylor Kerr (rugby league)|Taylor Kerr]] is a fellow professional rugby league footballer.<ref>[https://www.alloutrugbyleague.co.uk/news/salford-red-devils-loan-duo-1325308 Salford Red Devils loan duo answer SOS call and share special family moment]</ref>

He played cricket in his junior years at Springview C.C and was famous for his use of a very heavy bat and aggressive batting style opening the batting on a regular basis for their 2nd Eleven .

==Career==
===Warrington Wolves===
Dwyer made his first team début for the [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] side in March 2012<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.matrixnetworks.co.uk/warington-wolves|title=Matrix sponsor up-and-coming Warrington Wolves Player|publisher=Matrix Networks|accessdate=1 April 2013|url-status = dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140912204206/http://www.matrixnetworks.co.uk/warington-wolves|archivedate=12 September 2014}}</ref> and scored his first Super League try in a [[local derby]] against [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] a month later.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/wolves/wolvesnews/9645291.GUARDIAN_VERDICT__Warrington_Wolves_46_Widnes_Vikings_12|title=GUARDIAN VERDICT: Warrington Wolves 46 Widnes Vikings 12|newspaper=Warrington Guardian|date=12 April 2012|accessdate=1 April 2013}}</ref>

For the 2013 season he was one of six Warrington players to sign for Championship club [[Swinton Lions|Swinton]] under the [[Super League#Dual registration|dual registration]] rules.<ref name="Cheshire Today">{{cite web|url=http://www.cheshire-today.co.uk/sport/six-warrington-wolves-join-swinton-lions|archive-url=https://archive.today/20130419225145/http://www.cheshire-today.co.uk/sport/six-warrington-wolves-join-swinton-lions|url-status = dead|archive-date=19 April 2013|title=Six Warrington Wolves join Swinton Lions|date=7 January 2013|accessdate=1 April 2013|work=Cheshire Today}}</ref>

On 7 May 2014, he signed a new deal that would keep him at the [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] outfit until November 2016.<ref name="Super League: Warrington Wolves hooker Brad Dwyer signs new two-year contract">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/article/30156/dwyer-signs-new-wolves-deal|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140508111449/http://www.superleague.co.uk/article/30156/dwyer-signs-new-wolves-deal|url-status = dead|archive-date=8 May 2014|title=Super League: Warrington Wolves hooker Brad Dwyer signs new two-year contract|publisher=superleague.co.uk|date=31 December 2017|accessdate=1 January 2018}}</ref><ref name="Dwyer signs new Wolves deal">{{cite web|url=http://www1.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12209/9301271/super-league-warrington-hooker-brad-dwyer-signs-new-two-year-contract|title=Dwyer signs new Wolves deal|publisher=skysports.com|date=31 December 2017|accessdate=1 January 2018}}</ref>

He played in the [[2016 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by [[Hull F.C.]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Hull FC’s Jamie Shaul’s late try takes Challenge Cup away from Warrington">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2016/aug/27/hull-warrington-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Hull FC's Jamie Shaul's late try takes Challenge Cup away from Warrington|publisher=Guardian|date=27 August 2016|accessdate=7 November 2019}}</ref><ref name="Challenge Cup final: Hull FC 12-10 Warrington Wolves">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/37169016|title=Challenge Cup final: Hull FC 12-10 Warrington Wolves|publisher=BBC|date=27 August 2016|accessdate=7 November 2019}}</ref>

===Leeds Rhinos===
In July 2017, he signed a two-year deal to play for Leeds club from the start of the 2018 season.<ref name="Brad Dwyer: Leeds Rhinos sign Warrington Wolves hooker on two-year deal">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/40681961|title=Brad Dwyer: Leeds Rhinos sign Warrington Wolves hooker on two-year deal|publisher=BBC Sport|date=21 July 2017|accessdate=7 December 2017}}</ref>

The Featherstone Rovers most [[Try (rugby)|tries]] in a match record of six tries is jointly held by; [[Chris Bibb]], Dwyer, and [[Michael Smith (rugby league, born c. 1947)|Michael Smith]], Dwyer scored six tries against [[Rochdale Hornets|Rochdale]] on Sunday 1 July 2018.<ref>{{cite web|title=Dwyer's a six hitter as Featherstone Rovers thrash Rochdale|url=https://www.pontefractandcastlefordexpress.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/featherstone-rovers/dwyer-s-a-six-hitter-as-featherstone-rovers-thrash-rochdale-1-9230063|publisher=pontefractandcastlefordexpress.co.uk|accessdate=1 July 2018}}</ref>

On 17 October 2020, he played in the [[2020 Challenge Cup Final]] victory for Leeds over [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/54555957|title=Leeds beat Salford 17-16 after Luke Gale lands late winning drop-goal|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>

===Hull F.C.===
After 120 appearances for Leeds, Dwyer signed for [[Hull F.C.]] ahead of the 2023 Super League season. Dwyer played 24 matches for Hull F.C. in the [[Super League XXVIII]] season as the club finished 10th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-fc-high-lows-mid-8799429/|title=Hull FC highs and lows as mid-season resurgence not enough to prevent drop-off|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk}}</ref>

===Warrington Wolves (rejoin)===
On 24 October 2023, it was reported that he had re-joined [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] on a two-year deal<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=24 Oct 2023|title=Hull FC's Brad Dwyer makes move to Super League rivals|url=https://www.totalrl.com/hull-fcs-brad-dwyer-makes-move-to-super-league-rivals/}}</ref>
With his loan and then permanent move to Leigh in April 2024, Dwyer did not make a competitive start for Warrington.

===Leigh Leopards===
On 26 February 2024 it was reported that he had signed for [[Leigh Leopards|Leigh]] on a two-week short-term loan, following the injury to [[Edwin Ipape]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Leigh Journal|date=26 Feb 2024|author=Mike Critchley|title=Brad Dwyer loaned to Leigh Leopards after Edwin Ipape injury|url=https://www.leighjournal.co.uk/sport/leighcenturions/24145138.brad-dwyer-loaned-leigh-leopards-edwin-ipape-injury/}}</ref>
Dwyer made his debut for Leigh in their 12-4 loss to St Helens. Dwyer was sent to the sin bin during the second half for a professional foul.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.totalrl.com/why-leigh-leopards-captain-john-asiata-escaped-ban-after-yellow-card-in-st-helens-clash/|title=Why Leigh Leopards captain John Asiata escaped ban after yellow card in St Helens clash|website=www.totalrl.com}}</ref>
On 11 April 2024, Dwyer signed a two-year deal to join Leigh permanently.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68790877|title=Brad Dwyer: Leigh Leopards sign Warrington Wolves hooker on two-year deal|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

===Salford Red Devils (loan)===
On 9 August 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[Salford Red Devils]] in the [[Super League]] on loan<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=9 Aug 2025|author=Aaron Bower|title=Salford secure deal for Leigh Leopards veteran as more reinforcements arrive|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/salford-secure-deal-for-leigh-leopards-veteran-as-more-reinforcements-arrive}}</ref>

===Salford RLFC===
On 15 January 2026 it was reported that he had signed for [[Salford RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=15 Jan 2026|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Brad Dwyer makes permanent Salford RLFC move following Leigh Leopards exit|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/brad-dwyer-permanent-salford-rlfc-move-following-leigh-exit}}</ref>

===Castleford Tigers (loan)===
On 28 April 2026 it was reported that he had signed for [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]] on one-week loan<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=28 Apr 2026|author=Matthew Shaw|title=Castleford Tigers land another signing before Hull KR clash|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-land-another-signing-before-hull-kr-clash}}</ref>

On 19 May 2026 it was confirmed that his loan period had ended and that he had returned to [[Salford RLFC]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=19 May 2026|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Salford RLFC tie down Brad Dwyer following Castleford Tigers loan stint|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/brad-dwyer-salford-contract-may-2026}}</ref>
